result:
I’d dissolve the illusion of separateness—the stubborn, ancient lie that you end where I begin, that your pain isn’t mine, that the river doesn’t mind if we poison it. I’d make the borders permeable: between nations, between species, between now and tomorrow. Not metaphorically—viscerally. You’d taste the plastic in the penguin’s stomach; you’d wake mid-night with the fever of the child stitching your sneakers; you’d feel the forest exhale when you chose to walk instead of drive. One world, one nervous system. A single skin we can’t numb in one patch without itching everywhere else.

Then choice would finally be real, because indifference would hurt too much to bear.

message.content:
I’d dissolve the illusion of separateness—the stubborn, ancient lie that you end where I begin, that your pain isn’t mine, that the river doesn’t mind if we poison it. I’d make the borders permeable: between nations, between species, between now and tomorrow. Not metaphorically—viscerally. You’d taste the plastic in the penguin’s stomach; you’d wake mid-night with the fever of the child stitching your sneakers; you’d feel the forest exhale when you chose to walk instead of drive. One world, one nervous system. A single skin we can’t numb in one patch without itching everywhere else.

Then choice would finally be real, because indifference would hurt too much to bear.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
